name: Smith Machine Squat
resistance_profile: Fixed vertical bar path lets you set the feet forward and load the quads through a stable, controlled squat.
cues: Feet slightly forward to bias the quads
Sit straight down with knees tracking over the toes
Full-foot pressure, drive through the mid-foot
execution_steps: Set the bar across your upper traps under the safety hooks and place your feet slightly forward of the bar.
Unrack by rotating the bar off the hooks, take a breath, and brace your core.
Sit down and back, bending the knees to lower under control to at least parallel.
Keep the whole foot planted and drive up through the mid-foot to stand.
At the top, rotate the bar back onto the hooks to re-rack.
good_when: You want a quad-biased squat with a stable, guided bar path
Learning the squat pattern or training without a spotter
You want to push closer to failure safely
bad_when: You want to train balance and free-weight stabilizers
The fixed vertical path irritates your knees or hips
common_errors: Placing the feet too far forward and turning it into a leg press
progressions: Add pause reps at the bottom
regressions: Squat to a box or to parallel only
Lower the load and slow the tempo
